Anchors: 0 = User is fully known during the interaction; 1 = Full anonymity where the user remains unknowable during the interaction
0 = online- and offline-self are completely identical; 1 = feeling like ‘those online behaviors “aren’t me at all.”’ (Suler, 2004)
0 = one feels no responsibility for one’s actions, temporary suspension of moral cognitive processes; 1 = maximal feeling of responsibility for one’s actions
0 = text-only, minimal interpersonal cues; 1 = audio, video and nonverbal feedback, maximal nonverbal, paraverbal and verbal cues
0 = not concerned about impression on others, 0.3 = mildly concerned about impression on others, 1 = maximal concerned about impression on others
0 = maximal inhibition and fear of expressing oneself; 1 = no fear of expressing oneself
5-point likert scale; 1 = strongly disagree to 5 = strongly agree for the items of the MOD
-0.1 = no inclination to “say and do things in cyberspace that they wouldn’t ordinarily say and do in the face-to-face world” (Suler, 2004); 1.2 = maximal inclination toward such behavior
0 = no sentence containing any curse word; 1 = every sentence contains at least one curse word
